comment.body:
you allocate your money... like 70% bonds, 20% stocks.
you calculate it in a way that those 70% bonds exposure to give you "hedge" against inflation..
the rest 20% will produce the return.
you choose companies that you are not afraid to hold even if you don't see price chart for a month long...
reducing risk.

comment.body:
now your only job is to keep this balance.
when there is 5% plus on your stock holdings, you sell some to get the ratio again.
so is with bond weights... monthyl, yearly, quarterly rebalancing, depending on the volatility you took
